title: Dense Polish Pork Stew
description: A smoky, glassy-jelly one-pot pork stew that blends the deep flavors of smoked paprika, apple cider, and slow sous-vide braising for a sugar-free, hearty meal.
instructions: Preheat a sous-vide water bath to 68°C.
Season the pork cubes with salt and pepper, then seal them in a vacuum bag with thyme and bay leaf.
Cook the sealed pork in the sous-vide bath for 90 minutes.
Remove the bag, pat the pork dry, and heat a skillet over high heat to sear each side of the pork until golden brown.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Pour in the apple cider, sprinkle smoked paprika, and stir to combine all flavors.
Reduce the liquid over medium heat until it thickens into a glassy-jelly consistency, about 15 minutes.
Stir in the sugar-free sweetener to balance acidity and add a subtle sweetness.
Serve the dense stew hot, optionally garnished with fresh thyme leaves.
